rubric: Lists at least 5 foundational self-improvement methods (e.g., STaR, Reflexion, Voyager, DSPy, ADAS)
Names at least 4 key researchers or labs driving the field (e.g., Shinn, Hu, Stanford NLP, Sakana AI)
Lists at least 6 open-source frameworks or tools for building self-evolving agents
Covers at least 3 academic papers from 2024-2026 with venue information
Identifies at least 3 commercial companies building self-evolving agent products
Includes at least 2 benchmark datasets or evaluation frameworks for measuring agent self-improvement
Covers at least 3 distinct evolution mechanisms (e.g., prompt evolution, skill library, weight updates, code generation)
Names at least 2 projects released or significantly updated in the last 6 months
Includes at least 1 Chinese-language source or Chinese platform project
Lists at least 3 design patterns used across multiple self-evolving agent projects
Includes GitHub star counts or citation counts for at least 5 mentioned projects
Covers both LLM-based and RL-based approaches to agent self-improvement
Mentions at least 2 conference workshops or tracks dedicated to self-evolving agents (2024-2026)
Includes a timeline covering at least 3 milestones from 2022 to 2026
Names at least 1 meta-agent-search or automated architecture discovery approach
Compares at least 3 evolution mechanisms and states one tradeoff for each
Identifies at least 1 directional trend in the field with evidence from multiple sources
Explains why prompt/workflow evolution has gained traction over weight-update approaches for agent improvement
States a recommendation for which framework to use depending on use case (research vs production)
Identifies at least 2 open problems or areas where experts disagree
Provides a taxonomy or categorization framework that organizes the landscape into at least 3 segments
Discusses at least 2 risks or failure modes of self-evolving agents (e.g., reward hacking, capability regression)
Every mentioned project or paper includes a URL or citation link
Includes an explicit section for gaps or unknowns that the search did not cover
Uses sources from at least 3 distinct source types (GitHub, academic, blog, commercial, community)
